subject: Science
chapter_name: The World of Metals and Non-Metals
overall_learning_objectives: Identify common metals and non-metals used in daily life
Understand basic physical properties like luster, hardness, and state
Differentiate between metals and non-metals based on appearance and feel
Recognize the importance of metals in making tools and utensils
Learn about the conductivity of heat and electricity in simple terms
Understand the concept of rusting as a change in metals
Identify non-metals essential for life (like Oxygen)

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Materials
topics_covered: Hardness: Can it be compressed or scratched?
student_activities: Classify a list of 10 items as shiny or dull
Touch and feel different surfaces (Aluminium foil vs. Paper)

lesson_plan.title: Physical Properties: Luster and Hardness
topics_covered: Strength of metals vs. brittleness of non-metals
Exceptions (like Sodium being soft - brief mention)
student_activities: Rub a rusted nail with sandpaper to see the shine
Try to break a piece of charcoal vs. a copper wire

lesson_plan.title: Malleability and Ductility
teaching_strategy: Visual aids showing wires and foils
student_activities: Observe the flattening of a small lead or aluminium piece
List uses of foils in food packaging

lesson_plan.title: Heat and Electrical Conductivity
topics_covered: Why cooking pans have plastic handles
teaching_strategy: Heat conduction story (Spoon in hot tea)
student_activities: Test different materials in a simple circuit to see if the bulb glows
Identify which parts of an electric plug are metal
assessment: Exit slip: 'Why is copper used in wires?'

lesson_plan.title: Sonority and States of Matter
topics_covered: Mercury as a liquid metal (General awareness)
teaching_strategy: Sound test (Dropping a metal key vs. a wooden block)
student_activities: Listen to the sounds produced by different objects

lesson_plan.title: Common Uses and Rusting
topics_covered: Uses of Iron, Gold, and Copper
Uses of non-metals (Oxygen for breathing, Iodine as antiseptic)
teaching_strategy: Showing a rusted object vs. a new one
student_activities: Identify rusted items in the school/home garden
Make a poster on 'Metals in my Kitchen'

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Summary
student_activities: Participate in a 'Metal or Non-Metal' mystery bag game

teaching_aids: Samples of Copper, Iron, Aluminium, Coal, and Sulphur
teacher_tip: Keep the focus on observable physical properties. At this level, avoid complex chemical equations. Use the 'Kitchen Tour' approach—most metals and non-metals they need to know are right there in the cooking area!
